messages.content:
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the System worksheet
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            Exit For ' Exit the loop since we found at least one match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Show result based on the search
    If found Then
        MsgBox "Value found", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub

This VBA code defines a subroutine called `searchValue` that prompts the user to input a value and then searches for that value in the "System" worksheet of the current workbook. The search is case-insensitive and looks for partial matches within cell values.

Here's a brief explanation of the code:
1. The code declares variables for the worksheet, search value, cell, and a boolean flag to indicate if the value was found.
2. The user is prompted to input a value using an input box.
3. If the input is empty, a message is displayed, and the subroutine exits.
4. The search value is converted to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison.
5. The code loops through each cell in the used range of the "System" worksheet and uses `InStr` function to search for the input value (case-insensitive partial match).
6. If a match is found, the flag is set to true, and the loop is exited.
7. Based on the search result, a message box is displayed indicating whether the value was found or not.

You can run this subroutine in the VBA editor to search for a specific value within the "System" worksheet of your Excel workbook.
for this code i want to copy value found on Sheets3(System) to  Sheets2(Licente)

To modify the existing code to copy the found value from the "System" worksheet to the "Licence" worksheet, you can add the necessary code after the message box that confirms the value was found. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicence As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licence worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("System")
    Set wsLicence = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licence")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            ' Copy the found value to the Licence worksheet
            cell.Copy wsLicence.Range("A1") ' Assuming you want to paste in cell A1 of Licence sheet
            Exit For ' Exit the loop since we found at least one match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Show result based on the search
    If found Then
        MsgBox "Value found and copied to Licence sheet", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, after finding the value in the "System" worksheet, it copies the value to the "Licence" worksheet in cell A1. You can modify the destination cell as needed by changing the destination range in the `cell.Copy` line.

Run this modified subroutine in the VBA editor to search for a value in the "System" worksheet and copy it to the "Licence" worksheet if found.

Sure, I'll modify the code to copy the found value from Sheet3 (System) and paste it in Sheet2 (Licente) while maintaining the original location of the found value in Sheet3. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licence worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            ' Copy the found value from System sheet
            cell.Copy wsLicente.Range(cell.Address) ' Paste in the same cell address in Licente sheet
            Exit For ' Exit the loop since we found at least one match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Show result based on the search
    If found Then
        MsgBox "Value found and copied to Licente sheet", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

Now, when a value is found in Sheet3 (System), this code will copy that value and paste it into the same location in Sheet2 (Licente). Run this updated subroutine in the VBA editor to perform the search and maintain the location of the found value when copying to the Licente sheet.
copy all values found in System to Licente, not only first

To copy all values found in the "System" sheet to the "Licente" sheet, you can modify the code to loop through all the cells where the search value is found and copy each of them to the corresponding locations in the "Licente" sheet. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyCell As Range
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
        Next copyCell
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

This code will now search for the input value in the "System" sheet, identify all cells where the value is found, and copy all those values to the corresponding locations in the "Licente" sheet. Run this modified subroutine in the VBA editor to copy all values found in the System sheet to the Licente sheet.
on now change colour on Licente of cells with  founded value to green

To change the color of the cells in the "Licente" sheet that contain the found values to green, you can modify the code to apply a green fill color to those cells. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyCell As Range
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet and change color to green
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Interior.Color = RGB(0, 255, 0) ' Set green fill color
        Next copyCell
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet and highlighted in green.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, after copying the found values from the "System" sheet to the "Licente" sheet, the code iterates over the cells in the `copyRange` and sets the interior color of those cells to green using the `RGB` function. Run this modified subroutine in the VBA editor to copy all values found in the System sheet to the Licente sheet and highlight them in green.
ok after  now change value of copied cells on  Licente with an x mark

To add an "X" mark to the copied cells in the "Licente" sheet after highlighting them with a green color, you can modify the code to update the cell values with an "X" in addition to changing the cell color. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyCell As Range
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet and highlight cells in green
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Interior.Color = RGB(0, 255, 0) ' Set green fill color
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, highlighted in green, and marked with 'X'.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In the modified code, after copying the cells and applying a green fill color, it updates the cell values in the "Licente" sheet with an "X" mark. Run this subroutine in the VBA editor to copy all values found in the System sheet, highlight them in green, and mark them with an "X" in the Licente sheet.
for all cell that was copied change value to x not for firs

To change the value of all copied cells in the "Licente" sheet to "X" without considering the first found value, you can modify the code to update the values of all cells within the `copyRange` except for the first cell. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim firstCell As Boolean
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    firstCell = True ' Set flag to True for first found value
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If firstCell Then
                firstCell = False
            Else
                If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                    Set copyRange = cell
                Else
                    Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
                End If
                found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet and highlight cells in green
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Interior.Color = RGB(0, 255, 0) ' Set green fill color
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, highlighted in green, and marked with 'X'.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated version, the code sets a `firstCell` flag to distinguish the first found value and skips updating its value to "X". All subsequent found values will have their cell value changed to "X" in the Licente sheet. Run this revised subroutine in the VBA editor to perform the updated functionality.
ok now colour all cells for the colums from Licente where was copied data to an random color everytime i make an search

To change the color of all cells in the columns where data was copied in the "Licente" sheet to a random color every time a new search is made, you can modify the code to randomly generate a color for those columns. Here's the updated code with random color generation for the columns:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim firstCell As Boolean
    Dim col As Integer
    Dim rng As Range
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim cellColor As Integer
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If

    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    firstCell = True ' Set flag to True for first found value
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If firstCell Then
                firstCell = False
            Else
                If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                    Set copyRange = cell
                Else
                    Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
                End If
                found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet and highlight cells in green
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate a color for the columns with copied data
        For Each cell In wsLicente.UsedRange
            If Not Intersect(cell, copyRange) Is Nothing Then
                col = cell.Column
                randomColor = Int((16777216 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) ' Generate a random color
                wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
            End If
        Next cell
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, marked with 'X', and columns in random colors.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this revised code, after copying the values to the "Licente" sheet and marking them with an "X", the code randomly generates a color for the columns where the data was copied. Each time a new search is made, the columns with copied data will be highlighted in a different random color. Run this updated subroutine in the VBA editor to see the new functionality.
If Not Intersect(cell, copyRange) Is Nothing Then method intersect of global failed

The error relating to the `Intersect` method occurs because VBA does not directly support checking for intersections between ranges in the way that was attempted in the code. 

To achieve the desired functionality of applying a random color to the columns where data was copied in the "Licente" sheet, you can modify the code to loop through the cells in the copied range and then change the color of the entire column using the column number. Here's the updated code that resolves the issue:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim firstCell As Boolean
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim col As Integer
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    firstCell = True ' Set flag to True for first found value
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If firstCell Then
                firstCell = False
            Else
                If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                    Set copyRange = cell
                Else
                    Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
                End If
                found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet and mark with 'X'
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate a color for the columns with copied data
        randomColor = Int((16777216 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) ' Generate a random color
        For Each cell In copyRange
            col = cell.Column
            wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
        Next cell
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, columns marked with 'X', and painted with random color.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, instead of using `Intersect`, the column coloring is achieved by looping through the cells in the copied range and applying a random color to the entire column where each copied cell belongs. Run this modified subroutine in the VBA editor to copy values, mark them with 'X', and color the corresponding columns in a random color.
for all columns where data was copied in Licente random colours

To assign random colors to all columns where data was copied in the "Licente" sheet, you can modify the code to keep track of the columns where data was copied and then apply random colors to those columns. Here's the updated code:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyData As Collection
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim col As Integer
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range and collection for copy data
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    Set copyData = New Collection
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
            If Not copyData.Exists(copyCell.Column) Then
                copyData.Add copyCell.Column, CStr(copyCell.Column)
            End If
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate color for each unique column with copied data
        For Each col In copyData
            randomColor = Int((16777216 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) ' Generate a random color
            wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
        Next col
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, marked with 'X', and columns painted with random colors.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, the columns with copied data are tracked using a collection `copyData`, which stores the unique column numbers where data was copied. Random colors are then applied to each of these columns in the "Licente" sheet. Run this revised subroutine in the VBA editor to perform the operation.
For Each col In copyData must be variant or object

Apologies for the oversight. When iterating over the items in a collection in VBA, the loop variable should be declared as an object. Here's the corrected code snippet with the loop variable declared as an object:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyData As Collection
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim col As Object
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range and collection for copy data
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    Set copyData = New Collection
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
        End If
    Next cell
    
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
            If Not copyData.Exists(copyCell.Column) Then
                copyData.Add copyCell.Column, CStr(copyCell.Column)
            End If
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate color for each unique column with copied data
        For Each col In copyData
            randomColor = Int((16777216 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) ' Generate a random color
            wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
        Next col
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, marked with 'X', and columns painted with random colors.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

If Not copyData.Exists(copyCell.Column) Then-object does not support this property and error

I apologize for the confusion. In VBA, a Collection object does not have a built-in method like `.Exists` to check if a key exists. To work around this limitation, we can use a Dictionary object instead. Here's the revised code using a Dictionary for checking the existence of a column before adding it:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyData As Object ' Declare as Object for Dictionary
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim col As Variant ' Loop variable for columns
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range and collection for copy data (use Dictionary for key existence check)
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    Set copyData = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") ' Create a new Dictionary object
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            
            ' Check and add the column to the dictionary if it doesn't exist
            If Not copyData.exists(cell.Column) Then
                copyData.Add cell.Column, cell.Column
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
        
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate color for each unique column with copied data
        For Each col In copyData.Keys
            randomColor = Int((16777216 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) ' Generate a random color
            wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
        Next col
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, marked with 'X', and columns painted with random colors.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this revised code, a Dictionary object is used to store the unique column numbers where data was copied. This structure allows for efficient checking of column existence before adding it to the collection. Run this updated code in the VBA editor to copy values, mark them with 'X', and assign random colors to the corresponding columns in the "Licente" sheet.

Here's the updated code with the exclusion of white color:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyData As Object ' Declare as Object for Dictionary
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim col As Variant ' Loop variable for columns
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range and collection for copy data (use Dictionary for key existence check)
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    Set copyData = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") ' Create a new Dictionary object
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            
            ' Check and add the column to the dictionary if it doesn't exist
            If Not copyData.exists(cell.Column) Then
                copyData.Add cell.Column, cell.Column
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
        
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate color (excluding white) for each unique column with copied data
        For Each col In copyData.Keys
            Do
                randomColor = Int((16777215 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) + 1 ' Generate a random color (excluding white) 
            Loop Until randomColor <> 16777215 ' Continue generating until a non-white color is obtained
            wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
        Next col
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, marked with 'X', and columns painted with random colors (excluding white).", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```

In this updated code, the random color generation loop utilizes a `Do...Loop` structure to keep generating colors until a color other than white is obtained. The condition `Loop Until randomColor <> 16777215` ensures that white (16777215) is excluded from the generated colors. To change the color of all cells in the "Licente" sheet where data was found during the search to yellow, you can loop through the used range of the "Licente" sheet and apply the yellow color to those cells. Here's the updated code with this modification:

```vb
Sub searchValue()
    Dim wsSystem As Worksheet
    Dim wsLicente As Worksheet
    Dim searchValue As String
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim found As Boolean
    Dim copyRange As Range
    Dim copyData As Object ' Declare as Object for Dictionary
    Dim copyCell As Range
    Dim randomColor As Long
    Dim col As Variant ' Loop variable for columns
    
    ' Reference to the System and Licente worksheets
    Set wsSystem = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet3")
    Set wsLicente = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet2")
    
    ' Prompt for user input
    searchValue = InputBox("Input a value:", "Search in System")
    
    ' Check for empty input
    If searchValue = "" Then
        MsgBox "No value entered. Operation terminated.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    found = False ' Initialize the found flag to False
    
    ' Convert searchValue to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    searchValue = LCase(searchValue)
    
    ' Initialize the copy range and collection for copy data (use Dictionary for key existence check)
    Set copyRange = Nothing
    Set copyData = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") ' Create a new Dictionary object
    
    ' Scan through each cell in the used range of the System sheet
    For Each cell In wsSystem.UsedRange
        ' Use InStr for a case-insensitive partial match search. InStr returns 0 if no match is found
        If InStr(1, LCase(cell.Value2), searchValue, vbTextCompare) > 0 Then
            If copyRange Is Nothing Then
                Set copyRange = cell
            Else
                Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, cell)
            End If
            found = True ' Set found flag to True if there's a match
            
            ' Check and add the column to the dictionary if it doesn't exist
            If Not copyData.exists(cell.Column) Then
                copyData.Add cell.Column, cell.Column
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
        
    ' Copy all found values to the Licente sheet and mark with 'X'
    If found Then
        For Each copyCell In copyRange
            copyCell.Copy wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address)
            wsLicente.Range(copyCell.Address).Value = "X" ' Add "X" to copied cells
        Next copyCell
        
        ' Randomly generate color (excluding white) for each unique column with copied data
        For Each col In copyData.Keys
            Do
                randomColor = Int((16777215 - 1 + 1) * Rnd) + 1 ' Generate a random color (excluding white) 
            Loop Until randomColor <> 16777215 ' Continue generating until a non-white color is obtained
            wsLicente.Columns(col).Interior.Color = randomColor ' Apply random color to the entire column
        Next col
        
        ' Copy the search value to cell A2 in Licente
        wsLicente.Range("A2").Value = searchValue
        
        ' Change the color of all found cells in Licente to yellow
        For Each cell In wsLicente.UsedRange
            If cell.Value = searchValue Then
                cell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Yellow color
            End If
        Next cell
        
        MsgBox "All values found copied to Licente sheet, marked with 'X', columns painted with random colors (excluding white), search value copied to cell A2, and found cells in Licente colored yellow.", vbInformation
    Else
        MsgBox "Value not found", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub
```
